title: androsace gorodkovii subsp. semiperennis
intro: Androsace gorodkovii subsp. semiperennis, also known as the Himalayan Androsace, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the Himalayas. It is a type of alpine plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and treating respiratory infections, as well as having antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the Himalayan Androsace can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which is used to treat various ailments, including colds and flu.
